Test Scenarios: US1. As an Everfit Admin/Employee, I want to enable or disable the Booking feature for specific workspaces so that I can manage feature access in the first stage
Navigate to WS Detail and toggle Booking ON
Step:
1. Log in to CMS as Everfit Admin/Employee
2. Navigate to the Workspace Detail page for a target workspace
3. Scroll to the Features grid section
4. Verify a toggle card labeled "Booking" is displayed at the bottom of the grid
5. Verify the Booking toggle is currently OFF
6. Click the Booking toggle to turn it ON
7. Verify a "Success" toast appears and the toggle displays as ON
Test Data:
CMS Admin/Employee account.
Target workspace with Booking toggle currently OFF.
Expected:
- Booking toggle card visible at the bottom of the Features grid
- Toggle is clickable (can turn ON/OFF)
- After clicking toggle, it displays as "ON"
- "Success" toast appears immediately after toggle state change
- Workspace configuration is updated
- Booking menu becomes visible to all Coaches in that Workspace

Test Scenarios: Verify coach sees Booking menu after feature enabled
Step:
1. Ensure Booking feature is toggled ON for the workspace
2. Log in as a Coach in the same workspace
3. Navigate to the main application
4. Verify the Booking menu item is visible in the left-side navigation
5. Click the Booking menu item
6. Verify the Booking page loads successfully
Test Data:
Coach account in the target workspace.
Booking feature toggled ON for the workspace.
Expected:
- Booking menu item visible in left-side navigation for the Coach
- Coach can click and access Booking feature pages
- Booking page loads without errors

Test Scenarios: Refresh and navigate back to verify ON state persists
Step:
1. Ensure Booking toggle is set to ON from previous test
2. Refresh the CMS page
3. Navigate to the Workspace Detail page
4. Scroll to the Features grid section
5. Verify the Booking toggle still shows ON
6. Navigate away to another CMS page
7. Navigate back to the Workspace Detail page
8. Verify the Booking toggle still shows ON
Test Data: Booking toggle previously set to ON.
Expected:
- After page refresh, Booking toggle displays as ON
- After navigating away and returning, Booking toggle displays as ON
- Toggle state is persisted correctly across page refresh and navigation

Test Scenarios: Toggle Booking OFF and verify feature hidden for coaches
Step:
1. Navigate to the Workspace Detail page in CMS
2. Verify the Booking toggle is currently ON
3. Click the Booking toggle to turn it OFF
4. Verify a "Success" toast appears and the toggle displays as OFF
5. Log in as a Coach in the same workspace
6. Verify the Booking menu is NOT visible in left-side navigation
7. Toggle Booking back ON and verify existing booking data is still available
Test Data:
Workspace with Booking previously ON and existing booking data.
Coach account in the same workspace.
Expected:
- Toggle changes to OFF state with Success toast
- Booking feature on Coach Web App is hidden
- Booking menu not visible for Coaches
- Workspace data in Booking scope is persisted in the database (not deleted)
- Re-enabling Booking restores access to previous data

Test Scenarios: Refresh and navigate back to verify OFF state persists
Step:
1. Ensure Booking toggle is set to OFF from previous test
2. Refresh the CMS page
3. Navigate to the Workspace Detail page
4. Scroll to the Features grid section
5. Verify the Booking toggle shows OFF
6. Navigate away to another CMS page
7. Navigate back to the Workspace Detail page
8. Verify the Booking toggle still shows OFF
Test Data: Booking toggle previously set to OFF.
Expected:
- After page refresh, Booking toggle displays as OFF
- After navigating away and returning, Booking toggle displays as OFF
- Toggle state is persisted correctly across page refresh and navigation

Test Scenarios: US2. As an Owner or Admin of a Workspace that has Booking feature enabled, I want to access the Booking feature so that I can use this feature
Booking menu visible with BETA tag and hover dropdown
Step:
1. Log in as Workspace Owner or Admin with Booking feature enabled
2. Verify the Booking item is displayed in the left-side navigation
3. Verify a "BETA" tag is displayed next to the Booking label
4. Hover over the Booking menu item
5. Verify background highlight appears on hover
6. Verify contextual dropdown menu displays with "Calendar" and "Session Type" options
Test Data:
Owner or Admin account.
Workspace with Booking feature enabled.
Expected:
- Booking menu item visible in left-side navigation
- "BETA" tag displayed next to the Booking label
- Background highlight appears on hover
- Contextual dropdown menu shows "Calendar" and "Session Type" options

Test Scenarios: Click Booking first visit navigates to Calendar default
Step:
1. Clear browser cookies for Booking navigation history
2. Log in as Workspace Owner or Admin with Booking feature enabled
3. Click the Booking menu in the left navigation
4. Verify the system navigates to the Calendar page
5. Verify URL matches /home/booking/calendar?mode=(day|week|month)&start-date=YYYY-MM-DD&end-date=YYYY-MM-DD
6. Verify Calendar is marked as the active menu item within Booking
7. Verify the Booking submenu is expanded with Calendar visually highlighted
Test Data:
Owner or Admin account.
No Booking navigation cookie exists.
Booking feature enabled.
Expected:
- System navigates to Calendar page by default
- Calendar marked as active menu item within Booking
- Booking submenu is expanded
- Calendar is visually highlighted
- Browser cookie stores Calendar as last visited Booking page
- URL format: /home/booking/calendar?mode=(day|week|month)&start-date=YYYY-MM-DD&end-date=YYYY-MM-DD

Test Scenarios: Click Booking return visit restores last visited page
Step:
1. Navigate to the Session Type page within Booking feature
2. Verify browser cookie stores Session Type as last visited page
3. Navigate away from the Booking feature to another section
4. Click the Booking menu in the left navigation
5. Verify the system navigates to Session Type page (last visited)
6. Verify Booking submenu is expanded with Session Type highlighted
Test Data:
Owner or Admin account.
Previous visit to Session Type page stored in browser cookie.
Booking feature enabled.
Expected:
- System automatically navigates to the last visited Booking page (Session Type)
- Booking submenu is expanded
- Session Type is visually highlighted as active
- Cookie-based navigation restoration works correctly

Test Scenarios: Verify Session Type page URL and tab navigation
Step:
1. Navigate to the Session Type page via the Booking submenu
2. Verify URL matches app.everfit.io/booking/session-types?tab=active
3. Verify the Active tab is displayed and selected by default
4. Click the Archived tab
5. Verify URL updates to app.everfit.io/booking/session-types?tab=archived
6. Verify the Archived tab content is displayed
Test Data:
Owner or Admin account.
Booking feature enabled.
Existing session types in both active and archived states.
Expected:
- Session Type page URL shows ?tab=active by default
- Active tab is selected by default
- Clicking Archived tab updates URL to ?tab=archived
- Archived tab content displays correctly
- Tab switching works without page reload

Test Scenarios: Click Learn more about Booking opens help article
Step:
1. Navigate to the Booking section in left navigation
2. Verify the Booking submenu is expanded
3. Scroll to the bottom of the Booking submenu
4. Verify "Learn more about Booking" button is displayed
5. Click the "Learn more about Booking" button
6. Verify a new browser tab opens with the Booking help article page
Test Data:
Owner or Admin account.
Booking feature enabled.
Expected:
- "Learn more about Booking" button visible at the bottom of the Booking submenu
- Clicking the button opens a new browser tab
- New tab loads the Booking help article page
- Original application tab remains open and functional

Test Scenarios: Booking feature disabled hides menu and blocks URL access
Step:
1. Log in as Workspace Owner/Admin with Booking feature NOT enabled
2. Verify the Booking menu item is NOT visible in left-side navigation
3. Attempt to access Calendar page via direct URL /home/booking/calendar
4. Verify access is blocked or redirected
5. Attempt to access Session Type page via direct URL /booking/session-types
6. Verify access is blocked or redirected
Test Data:
Owner or Admin account.
Workspace with Booking feature disabled.
Expected:
- Booking menu navigation item is not visible in left-side navigation
- Direct URL access to Calendar page is blocked or redirected
- Direct URL access to Session Type page is blocked or redirected
- No Booking-related UI elements are visible

Test Scenarios: User terminology applied across Booking UI elements
Step:
1. Navigate to My Account settings
2. Set Client Terminology to "Athlete"
3. Set Coach Terminology to "Trainer"
4. Navigate to the Booking feature
5. Verify all occurrences of "client" display as "Athlete"
6. Verify all occurrences of "coach" display as "Trainer"
7. Check labels, field placeholders, helper text, empty states, tooltips, and notification messages
Test Data:
Owner or Admin account.
Client Terminology set to: "Athlete".
Coach Terminology set to: "Trainer".
Booking feature enabled.
Expected:
- All "client"/"clients" terms replaced with "Athlete"/"Athletes"
- All "coach"/"coaches" terms replaced with "Trainer"/"Trainers"
- Terminology applied consistently in labels, placeholders, helper text, empty states, tooltips, and messages
- No untranslated default terms remain in Booking UI

Test Scenarios: Update terminology reflects immediately without reload
Step:
1. Open the Booking feature page in one browser tab and note current terminology
2. Open My Account settings in another tab
3. Update Client Terminology from "Athlete" to "Member"
4. Switch back to the Booking feature tab without reloading
5. Verify "Athlete" is immediately replaced with "Member" across all UI elements
6. Update Coach Terminology from "Trainer" to "Instructor" in My Account tab
7. Switch to Booking tab and verify "Trainer" replaced with "Instructor"
Test Data:
Owner or Admin account.
Initial Client Terminology: "Athlete".
Updated Client Terminology: "Member".
Initial Coach Terminology: "Trainer".
Updated Coach Terminology: "Instructor".
Expected:
- Terminology updates reflected immediately without page reload
- All "Athlete" instances change to "Member" automatically
- All "Trainer" instances change to "Instructor" automatically
- No stale terminology visible after update
- Changes apply across labels, placeholders, helper text, empty states, tooltips, and messages
